mechanism_narrative: A persistent decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, directly lifting equity valuations and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate falls below 8.0 percent, labor market tightness accelerates wage growth and consumer spending, which boosts corporate revenue expectations and drives positive S&P 500 returns over the subsequent 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 5-day returns in more than 55% of instances where UNRATE drops below 8.0, or the predictive Rank IC falls below 0.02 over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in real GDP and residential housing starts signals broad-based economic expansion, which amplifies corporate profit margins and sustains positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-trading-day horizon.
failure_condition: The joint signal produces a negative or statistically insignificant Rank IC over any 12-month rolling window, or forward returns do not exceed the baseline median by at least 0.5%.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production combined with stable inflation indicates supply-side capacity expansion without demand-pull price pressures, which improves corporate margins and generates positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The mechanism fails to predict positive returns when CPIAUCSL rises above 3.0% year-over-year, or the forward return differential between triggered and non-triggered periods falls below 0.3%.

mechanism_narrative: An upward trajectory in retail and food services sales reflects strengthening consumer demand, which directly translates to higher corporate revenues and positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: The monthly RSAFS growth rate shows no statistically significant correlation with 5-day forward SPX returns over a 5-year sample, or the direction of the relationship flips sign in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate crosses above 0.5 percent, the Federal Reserve signals a normalization of monetary policy that compresses equity risk premiums and induces negative S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns remain positive in more than 60% of instances following the 0.5 percent threshold breach, or the predictive power vanishes when controlling for VIX movements.

mechanism_narrative: Rapid employment growth paired with a declining unemployment rate confirms a self-reinforcing labor market recovery, which sustains consumer spending and drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint employment signal yields a negative Rank IC over any 2-year rolling window, or forward returns do not significantly outperform the market median when PAYEMS growth exceeds 200,000 per month.

mechanism_narrative: In a high-inflation environment, a sharp deceleration in housing starts signals cooling aggregate demand, which reduces corporate pricing power and generates negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: The mechanism produces positive SPX returns in more than 50% of triggered instances, or the forward return differential becomes statistically indistinguishable from zero when CPIAUCSL exceeds 225.0.
